executive_summary: 10 countries tracked. 2 Red (Croatia, Germany). 7 Amber. 1 No Data (Singapore). Croatia remains blocked on ecosio deliverables. Germany has critical P0 customer issues piling up. KSA and Malaysia approaching Green trajectory.

trends.insight: Red for 4 consecutive reports. Blocked on ecosio sample payloads for e-reporting. March 20 regulatory deadline at risk without immediate unblock.
Red — P0 customer issues across HID Global, SGK, K&S. Print template mapping broken. Multiple field-level fixes needed.
Amber for 4 consecutive reports. Issue count remains high at 25+ across multiple clients.
Amber but improving. Support tickets trending down. Aramco PDF fix deployed.
Amber but stable. No major escalations. Unified API development on track.

predictions.prediction: If ecosio delivers sample payloads this week, March 20 deadline is still achievable. If delayed further, deadline will be missed.
On track to reach Green next report if no new support tickets emerge.
Will remain Red until P0 field fixes are deployed. Earliest improvement: next report if dedicated engineering focus maintained.

coaching: Two countries in Red (Croatia, Germany) — both blocked on different things. Croatia is an external dependency (ecosio), Germany is internal execution. Different unblocking strategies needed.
KSA and Malaysia are the closest to Green. Prioritizing their remaining items could give quick wins and demonstrate program momentum.
risk_watch: Croatia March 20 deadline — 30 days remaining. Currently blocked with no path forward until ecosio delivers.
Germany P0 backlog growing. Without dedicated engineering sprint, customer escalations will increase.
